The text content from the image is as follows:

"Figure 1 Architecture of CPT and the counterpart PTMs. Different from other PTMs, CPT consists of three parts: a shared encoder (S-Enc), an understanding decoder (U-Dec) and a generation decoder (G-Dec)."

Figure 2 Five ways to fine-tune CPT for text classification. “T1-4” and “P1-2” refer to text input x and prompt tokens, respectively. V(y) is the mapping function that maps the language model predictions to the label. [C] and [S] are abbreviations for [CLS] and [SEP], respectively.

The text content from the image is as follows:

Figure 3 Two examples of fine-tuning CPT for sequence labeling. "T1-4" and "L1-4" refer to text input x and token labels, respectively. Figure 3 Two examples of fine-tuning CPT for sequence labeling. “T1-4” and “L1-4” refer to text input x and token labels, respectively.

Figure 4 Two examples of fine-tuning CPT for Machine Reading Comprehension. “P1-3”, “Q1-2” refer to passages and questions, respectively.

Figure 5 Example of fine-tuning CPTg for Conditional Generation. “S1-4” and “T1-4” refer to input and target sequences, respectively.

The text content from the image is as follows:

"Figure 6 Inference throughput for BART and CPT. It is measured on the same parts of datasets that the models are evaluated. The beam size is 4 and the batch size is 8."
